title: Tyrone May

[[Terrell May]] (brother) }} }} '''Tyrone May''' (born 21 June 1996) is a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]. Primarily a {{rlp|so}}, May has played in a number of other positions during his career, including {{rlp|ce|sh|lf}} and {{rlp|fb}}. May previously played for the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[NRL]] with whom he won the [[2021 NRL Grand Final]] coming off the bench. ==Background== May was born in Sydney, Australia, to a Samoan mother and Australian father.{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/panthers/penrith-panthers-rookie-tyrone-may-works-hard-to-provide-the-xfactor/news-story/83c200d27ffa78a7faf15d490c7fa69f|title= Penrith Panther's rookie Tyrone May works hard to provide the X Factor|publisher= Daily Telegraph}} He was educated at [[Patrician Brothers' College, Blacktown]]. He played his junior rugby league for the [[Minchinbury Jets]] in the [[Penrith District Rugby League]]. May's younger brothers [[Taylan May|Taylan]] and [[Terrell May|Terrell]] are also professional rugby league players. ==Playing career== ===2017=== [[File:Tyrone May.jpg|thumb|upright|May playing for Penrith in 2017]] In round 19, May made his first grade debut in the Panthers 34–22 win over the [[New Zealand Warriors]] at [[Mount Smart Stadium]] scoring a try on debut.{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/cleary-stars-as-panthers-down-warriors/tabid/10874/newsid/109984/default.aspx|title=Cleary stars as Panthers down Warriors|date=14 July 2017|publisher=}} ===2018=== On 23 June, May made his international debut for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]], playing five-eighth in the 22–38 loss to Tonga, scoring a try. On 20 August, May was fined $1100 after he was charged by the NRL for punching an opponent in Penrith's loss to Newcastle.[https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/08/19/five-fined-for-fighting-in-melee-in-penrith-panthers-loss-to-newcastle-knights/ Five fined for fighting in melee in Penrith Panthers' loss to Newcastle Knights – NRL] ===2019=== On 5 March, May was stood down from playing by the [[NRL]] after being charged by NSW Police in relation to sex tapes the player filmed dating back to February 2018. May was charged with two counts which were filming and disseminating the footage without consent. May and Penrith [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] player Liam Coleman who is the son of former South Sydney player [[Craig Coleman]] were summoned to Penrith Police Station where May was granted bail and was ordered to appear at Penrith Local Court on 1 May 2019.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/03/05/tyrone-may-charged-by-nsw-police-with-two-video-offences/|title=May stood down after being charged with video offences|website=www.nrl.com |date=5 March 2019 }}{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/breaking-tyrone-may-arrested-over-nrl-sex-tape-scandal/news-story/0adda2102d42e9031e030494fc6d8ec3|title=Penrith Panthers utility Tyrone May arrested, charged and stood down over NRL sex tape scandal|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=5 March 2019}} [[File:Tyrone May Samoa.jpg|thumb|upright|May on international duty with Samoa at the 2021 RLWC]] On 16 July, May was hit with fresh charges at Parramatta Local Court by NSW Police bringing the total number of charges to eight. NSW Police laid additional counts of recording and distributing intimate images without consent against May.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/penrith-star-tyrone-may-hit-with-fresh-sex-tape-charges/news-story/3daf3b907d84ac21d6be7594698a7029/|title=Penrith star Tyrone May hit with fresh sex tape charges|website=Fox Sports|date=16 July 2019}} May faced a possible ban from the sport altogether if he was found guilty.{{Cite web|url=https://parlaygame.com/2019/03/05/nrl-player-can-be-banned-after-arrest-for-sex-tape-•-parlay-game/|title=NRL player can be banned after arrest for sex tape • Parlay Game {{!}} Parlay Game|language=en-US|access-date=11 March 2019}} On 22 November 2019, May pleaded guilty to four charges of intentionally recording an intimate image without consent. He appeared again at court for sentencing on 31 January 2020.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/11/22/tyrone-may-pleads-guilty-to-recording-intimate-images/|title=Tyrone May pleads guilty to recording intimate images|date=22 November 2019|website=National Rugby League|language=en|access-date=22 November 2019}} ===2020=== On 31 January, May narrowly avoided jail and was sentenced to 300 hours of community service plus a three-year good behaviour bond over sex tapes filmed without consent. Magistrate Robyn Denes spoke in the court to May saying "This behaviour is not only morally reprehensible, it's criminal, Ultimately, this is an offence which takes what was consensual sexual acts to another level. It does breach trust".{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-01-31/nrl-player-tyrone-may-sentenced/11916802/|title=NRL player Tyrone May sentenced to 300 hours of community service over sex tapes filmed without consent|newspaper=ABC News|date=30 January 2020}}{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/panthers/nrl-2020-panthers-tyrone-may-sentenced-panthers-on-the-prowl-community-program/news-story/b8bfcf265ee8ae760066b711a12c8fa1/|title='What an inappropriate name': Tyrone May magistrate tells Panthers to change community program|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=31 January 2020}} On 8 February, it was announced by the [[NRL]] that May would be suspended for the first four matches of the [[2020 NRL season]] and fined 25 percent of his 2019 salary which he earned at Penrith.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/rugby-league-players-association-deem-nrls-nofault-stand-down-policy-as-unfair-after-tyrone-may-receives-punishment/news-story/db1b55d0e74bde0147012d53a14d0413/|title=Rugby League Players' Association deem NRL's no-fault stand down policy as unfair after Tyrone May receives punishment|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=7 February 2020}} May played 16 games for Penrith in the [[2020 NRL season]] as the club won the [[Minor Premiership]]. He scored a try for Penrith in their preliminary final victory over South Sydney and was selected ahead of teammate [[Brent Naden]] for the [[2020 NRL Grand Final]]. Following [[Ryan Papenhuyzen]]'s try where he ran past May untouched, Melbourne raced out to a 26–0 lead in the grand final. May was substituted by coach [[Ivan Cleary]] and took no further part in the game as Penrith mounted a comeback but ultimately lost 26–20.{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2020/oct/25/nrl-grand-final-2020-penrith-panthers-v-melbourne-storm-teams-live-scores-winners-win-team-lists-news-kickoff-time-game-start-updates-anz-stadium/|title=Melbourne Storm beat Penrith Panthers as it happened|newspaper=The Guardian|date=25 October 2020|last1=Hytner|first1=Mike}} ===2021=== May played a total of 21 games for Penrith in the [[2021 NRL season]] including the club's [[2021 NRL Grand Final]] victory over [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/oct/04/one-of-finest-nrl-grand-finals-of-all-time-provides-cure-to-difficult-season/|title=One of finest NRL grand finals of all time provides cure to difficult season|website=www.theguardian.com|date=3 October 2021}} Following Penrith's grand final victory, May posted a story on both [[Instagram]] and [[Twitter]] saying "And the dirt that they threw on my name turned to soil and I grew up out it, Time for y'all to figure out what y'all gon' do about it. Love my brothers". After posting the story, several Penrith players also took to social media in support of May's message. May later removed the post from both social media platforms after it caused outrage from the general public, most notably from Fox Sports commentator [[Andrew Voss]] who said “Someone has to pull this bloke to one side and say, ‘What the hell? What world do you live in?, “He was an inch away from being sent to jail. And the support of the players. No less than 10 players came out (saying), ‘With you brother’ … and all that. Just keep it in-house, boys. I don't think it did Tyrone any favours at all". Lawyer and [[Wests Tigers]] chairman Lee Hagipantelis, who acted for a woman depicted in a video recorded by May, also reacted to the post. "I do hope that Tyrone doesn't think that a premiership victory somehow vindicates criminal behaviour, One only needs to have a look at the comments made by the magistrate when Tyrone was convicted to see how serious it was. It's something he should quite probably be quite embarrassed and ashamed of and not necessarily espouse or seek to justify, if that's what he was intending to do".{{cite news|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-star-tyrone-may-deletes-controversial-instagram-post-after-uproar/news-story/531026064a911e866b9849ec86e2c498/|title=NRL star Tyrone May deletes controversial Instagram post after uproar|newspaper=News.com.au|date=6 October 2021}}{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/tyrone-may-instagram-premiership-panthers-sex-tape/l5m32cnwosu1veownhivtyap/|title=Tyrone May criticised for referencing sex tape case in social media post|website=www.sportingnews.com.au|date=5 October 2021 }} On 19 October, May was stood down by Penrith and issued a breach notice by the NRL alleging he acted contrary to the best interests of the game for posting and being part of social media posts which do not align with the values of the game. The Breach Notice proposed May must complete further education programs as directed by the NRL. May was also issued with a proposed $7,500 fine.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-tyrone-may-stood-down-nathan-cleary-stephen-crichton-breach-notices-penrith-panthers-nrl-integrity-unit/news-story/b48b7f2bb8c2efebc1c9bc8e36b2838a/|title=May stood down as Panthers trio cop breach notices for post-GF celebrations|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=19 October 2021}} On 27 October, May was issued with a show cause notice by the Penrith club due to a serious breach in his playing contract which was in relation to his post on [[Instagram]] following the club's victory in the [[2021 NRL Grand Final]].{{cite web|url=https://7news.com.au/sport/rugby-league/tyrone-may-ordered-to-front-panthers-board-over-serious-breach-of-contract-c-4346423/|title=Tyrone May ordered to front Panthers board over 'serious breach' of contract|website=7news.com.au|date=27 October 2021}} On 3 November, May's contract was terminated by the Penrith Panthers board.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-tyrone-may-sacked-penrith-panthers-grand-final-instagram-post-board-meeting/news-story/59288733d305e2f4bb4f9ac478e9e3a4/|title=Panthers sack Premiership winner Tyrone May after Insta post|website=foxsports.com.au|date=3 November 2021}} On 1 December 2021, it was reported that May had signed for [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=1 Dec 2021|accessdate=2 Dec 2021|title=Catalans Dragons sign NRL Grand Final winner Tyrone May|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/catalans-dragons-sign-tyrone-may/}} ===2022=== In round 1 of the 2022 Super League season, May made his club debut for [[Catalans Dragons]] where they lost 28-8 against [[St Helens R.F.C.]]{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-beat-catalans-in-fiery-season-opener-talking-points/|title=St Helens beat Catalans in fiery season opener – talking points|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=10 February 2022 }} In June, May signed a one-year contract extension to remain at Catalans until the end of the 2023 season.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tyrone-may-extends-catalans-dragons-stay/|title=Tyrone May extends Catalans Dragons stay with new deal|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=14 June 2022 }} In October May was named in the [[2021_Men's_Rugby_League_World_Cup_squads#Samoa|Samoa squad]] for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup – Men's tournament|2021 Rugby League World Cup]].[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rugby-league-world-cup-2021-squad-lists/ Full list of every squad at the Rugby League World Cup 2021] ===2023=== On 21 August, May signed a two-year deal to join [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] starting in 2024.{{cite web|last1=Appleyard |first1=Joe |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/tyrone-may-catalans-hull-kr-8691429/|title=Tyrone May leaves Hull KR fans eager for more after standout display|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk |date=21 August 2023 }} On 14 October, May played in Catalans [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }} ===2024=== May played 28 matches for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2024 Super League season. On 12 October, May played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.{{cite web|last1=Calvert |first1=Lee |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com |date=12 October 2024 }} ===2025=== On 7 June, May played in Hull Kingston Rovers 8-6 Challenge Cup final victory over Warrington. It was the clubs first major trophy in 40 years.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk |date=8 June 2025 }} On 18 September, May played in and scored against Warrington on the last game of the regular season which would see Hull Kingston Rovers lift the League Leaders Shield {{cite web | title=Super League: Hull KR 28-20 Warrington Wolves: Robins win League Leaders' Shield | date=18 September 2025 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo }} On 9 October, May played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Super League Grand Final victory over Wigan.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete the treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk |date=11 October 2025 }} ===2026=== On 19 February, May played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[World Club Challenge]] victory against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live/cwy6dlgx3kyt/|title=Relive Hull KR's World Club Challenge win over Brisbane|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} == Statistics == {|
